author: mcclec07
name: Framework for Legal Score-First PPM-D Mixtures — Formal Proof + Two Valid Paths
blurb: Non-record methodology submission: formal proof that the previously reported PPM-D mixture BPB is invalid due to normalization defects, plus two architecturally valid correction paths. Path B now has an audited first-8M-token sliding-only subset result (mixture_bpb 1.5458598675878639; denominator_match=true; claim_ready=true), but no corrected full-validation or TTT Path B claim is made. Base model: 11-layer 512d SP8192, depth recurrence, INT6+brotli, TTT.
val_bpb_invalid_ppm_mixture_note: INVALID — see README.md for formal proof of normalization failure. This value is reported for reference only and should NOT be treated as a valid BPB.
val_bpb_note: No corrected full-validation PPM-D BPB has been computed yet. An audited first-8M-token sliding-only Path B subset result exists, but this submission makes no corrected full-validation or TTT Path B claim. See README.md.
path_b_subset_note: Audited first-8M-token sliding-only Path B subset result on the exp_1876 artifact. This is a corrected subset result, not a corrected full-validation contest claim; no TTT Path B result exists.
methodology_contributions: Formal proof that geometric-mean neural byte decomposition is not a valid probability distribution (Theorem 2)
Formal proof that PPM-D without exclusion does not normalize (Theorem 1 note)
Formal proof that the resulting mixture is not normalized (Theorem 3)
Verification that score-before-update ordering is correct (Theorem 4)
Verification that byte denominator is correct (Theorem 5)
Verification that distributed coverage is correct (Theorem 6)
Path A: Token-normalized PPM mixture design with C++ pybind11 backend (bit-exact equivalence, ~50x speedup, CUDA backend planned)

Path B: Byte-level trie marginalization with proper PPM-D exclusion and an audited first-8M-token sliding-only subset result (mixture_bpb 1.5458598675878639, neural_only_bpb 1.5619188191495212, ppm_d_only_bpb 2.118415681067613, denominator_match=true, claim_ready=true)
